sections.name: A
sections.title: SECTION A: Attempt All Questions (55 marks)

questions.text: 1) a) State the economic terms which mean the same as the following statements: i) The process of making commodities. (1 mark) ii) The process of using commodities. (1 mark) b) What do you understand by the phrase: i) Human wants are unlimited. (1 mark) ii) Human wants are Dynamic. (1 mark)

subQuestions.label: a) i)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: Production

subQuestions.label: a) ii)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: Consumption

subQuestions.label: b) i)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: There are too many human wants. No individual, society, or nation has ever satisfied all its wants.

subQuestions.label: b) ii)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: Human wants are not static; they keep changing with time, place, age, etc.

questions.text: 2) Explain how each of the following may affect demand for commodity A which is a Normal good: a) The level of income and its distribution. (2 marks) b) Population and its composition. (2 marks)

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: The level of income and its distribution.
subQuestions.correctAnswer: Other things being equal, the higher the level of income, the greater the quantity demanded. However, quantity demanded will be greater when income is evenly distributed. In regions where the level of income is low, the demand is also low, and vice versa.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: A big population size, other things being constant, will have a bigger level of demand, and vice versa. The composition of the population, i.e., the age, sex, determines quantity demanded. A younger population will have a higher level of demand than an aging population.

questions.text: 3 a) Given that the price of beans increases from 200 Frw to 250 Frw per kg and as a result, quantity demanded reduces from 8 to 6 kgs. Calculate the price elasticity of demand for beans. (2 marks) b) Explain your answer in 3(a) above. (2 marks)

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Calculate the price elasticity of demand for beans.
subQuestions.correctAnswer: % change in quantity demanded / % change in price = (-2/8) / (50/200) = -0.25 / 0.25 = 1

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: The price elasticity of demand is 1, meaning that the percentage change in quantity demanded is equal to the percentage change in price.

questions.text: 4 a) Define the following types of monopoly: i) Natural monopoly. (2 marks) ii) Collusive monopoly. (2 marks) b) What is the common feature of all forms of monopoly? (1 mark)

subQuestions.label: a) i)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: A natural monopoly occurs when the market is only big enough for a single seller.

subQuestions.label: a) ii)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: A collusive monopoly occurs when two or more firms collude or agree to work together to operate as a monopoly.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Common feature of all forms of monopoly.
subQuestions.correctAnswer: The common characteristic of monopolies is being the sole producer/supplier of a particular commodity.

questions.text: 5 a) What is meant by "Capital accumulation"? (1 mark) b) Explain any four determinants of capital accumulation. (4 marks)

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: Capital accumulation refers to the increase in the stock of capital goods of a country over a period of time.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: The level of saving, the level of incomes, cultural and institutional beliefs, the level of development of infrastructure, the wage rate, attitude of people towards work, economic climate, and investment opportunities.

questions.text: 6) Explain why the following may occur as a result of unemployment: a) Reduction in tax revenue. (3 marks) b) Increased government expenditure. (3 marks)

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: Reduction in tax revenue is one of the costs of unemployment. Government gets tax revenue by taxing the incomes people earn during employment. When a person becomes unemployed, they can no longer be taxed because they have no income.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: Increased government expenditures may be considered as one of the costs of unemployment. Normally, people without employment and income need support from the government in the form of free services like education for children, healthcare, and in extreme cases, shelter. This requires the government to spend more on these services when the level of unemployment increases.

questions.text: 7) a) Give any two regional economic groupings in Africa in which Rwanda is a member. b) For any of the bodies mentioned in 7(a) above, give any three of its economic achievements.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: EAC (East African Community), COMESA (Common Market for Eastern and Southern Africa)

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: Establishment of the East African Community Customs Union, Establishment of the East African Community Common Market, Convertibility of the currencies of Kenya, Tanzania, and Uganda.

questions.text: 8) Discuss the effects of taxation on the following: i) Consumption. ii) Prices. iii) Investment.

subQuestions.label: i)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: Direct and indirect taxes both affect consumption. Direct taxation imposes a direct effect on consumer spending capacity through its reduction of disposable income, leading to a fall in savings. Indirect taxes reduce the demand for goods and services. They reduce the real income of the consumer through their price effect.

subQuestions.label: ii)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: The direct taxes are normally imposed on income, so they can affect prices through demands for higher wages to maintain real earnings. The effect of indirect taxes on prices depends on the conditions of demand and supply facing the taxed activities or commodities. The price will increase if an indirect tax is imposed.

subQuestions.label: iii)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: Heavy and highly progressive taxes may reduce the ability and willingness to save and invest.

questions.text: 9) a) What is meant by "large scale agricultural production"? b) Identify any four characteristics of large scale agricultural production.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: Large scale agricultural production is where agricultural farms are big with a high level of mechanization and specialization.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Characteristics of large scale agricultural production.
subQuestions.correctAnswer: It is carried out on large plantations, the yields are high, equipment and machinery may be advanced and large scale, methods of production are usually scientific, a large number of workers are employed, and production is for a large market, domestic and foreign.

questions.text: 10) Mention any five factors that influence people’s standards of living in an economy.
questions.correctAnswer: General price level in an economy, level of income, national income distribution, level of education and skills, and quantity and quality of goods produced.

questions.text: 11) a) What do you understand by "Privatization"? b) Explain the following forms of privatization: i) Divestiture. ii) Leasing.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: Privatization is the transfer of government-owned enterprises to the private sector. It involves policies aimed at promoting private control and ownership of major enterprises and means of production.

subQuestions.label: b) i)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: Divestiture is when the government sells all its shares to the private sector.

subQuestions.label: b) ii)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: Leasing is when the government passes over the possession and management of a public enterprise to the private developer for a specified period of time.

sections.name: B
sections.title: SECTION B: Attempt any THREE questions (45 marks)

questions.text: 12) a) What is a Central Bank? b) Explain any eight functions of a Central Bank. c) Identify any five measures used by the Central Bank to promote rapid economic growth and development in an economy.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: A central bank is a financial institution whose aim is to control the quality and use of money in such a way as to facilitate the implementation of certain monetary policies.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: It has the right to issue and renew coins and notes, acts as a banker to the government, acts as a banker to commercial banks, is the manager and custodian of foreign currencies, keeps the funds of some government institutions and international organizations, uses the tools of monetary policy to influence the level of economic activity.

subQuestions.label: c)
subQuestions.text: Measures used by the Central Bank to promote rapid economic growth and development.
subQuestions.correctAnswer: It directs its policies towards economic growth and development, controls the quantity and composition of credit through selective credit control, may run schools and colleges for training bankers, controls the activities of foreign banks, tends to finance only activities which bring in profits quickly.

questions.text: 13) a) Define the following terms: i) Deflation. ii) Devaluation. b) Briefly explain how devaluation would result in the following: i) Increase in exports. ii) Protection of domestic industries. iii) Increase in employment. c) Account for any three effects of deflation.

subQuestions.label: a) i)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: Deflation is a continuous (persistent) decrease in price level.

subQuestions.label: a) ii)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: Devaluation is a purposeful action of the government to reduce the value of the domestic currency in terms of foreign currencies.

subQuestions.label: b) i)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: When domestic currency is devaluated, the price of exports declines, hence demand for exports increases because domestic products are really cheaper than before.

subQuestions.label: b) ii)
subQuestions.text: Devaluation and protection of domestic industries.
subQuestions.correctAnswer: When domestic currency is devaluated, imports become more expensive because we need more money to afford foreign products, therefore the demand for local products increases.

subQuestions.label: b) iii)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: Because of protection of domestic industries, production of exports is promoted, therefore the development of industries results in an increase in employment.

subQuestions.label: c)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: Production is discouraged, increase in unemployment, fall in income.

questions.text: 14) a) Explain briefly how the following determine economic growth: i) Stock of capital. ii) State of technology. iii) Market potential. b) Briefly explain how economic growth is good for an economy.

subQuestions.label: a) i)
subQuestions.text: Stock of capital and economic growth.
subQuestions.correctAnswer: When the capital accumulation is high, it encourages economic growth because it increases the productivity of the other factors of production.

subQuestions.label: a) ii)
subQuestions.text: State of technology and economic growth.
subQuestions.correctAnswer: When a country has advanced technology, that implies that it has better techniques and methods of production of goods and services.

subQuestions.label: a) iii)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: A big domestic and foreign market encourages investment and then production of goods and services, which leads to positive economic growth.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Economic growth is good for an economy.
subQuestions.correctAnswer: Increased employment, improvement in country’s balance of payment position, high life expectancy, economic growth expands the government's tax base, economic growth leads to urbanization and modernization.

questions.text: 15) a) Discuss the economic importance of a population census. b) Discuss the advantages of a growing population.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Economic importance of a population census.
subQuestions.correctAnswer: It helps to know the size of the population in each administrative unit, the growth rate of population, the quality of population in terms of education levels, the composition of the population, and the employment status.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: An increase in population leads to an increased supply of labor, expanding markets for consumer goods, an increase in the level of consumption and the number of taxpayers, a reduction in the average cost per person of providing social services and infrastructure, and a larger pool of people able to create a formidable defense force.

questions.text: 16) a) What are the phases followed in development planning? b) Discuss the reasons why plans may fail in the context of developing countries.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: Situation analysis, prioritizing key issues, developing objectives, developing plans to address the objectives, and developing budgets to achieve the plans.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Reasons why plans may fail in developing countries.
subQuestions.correctAnswer: Absence of reliable and adequate data, lack of skilled personnel, and lack of political will.
